summary: Iteration 6 frontend-only test of new compass-style turn-by-turn bar on /route/{id}/stop/{stopId}/navigate. Used seeded route 4fca5350-ddf4-40cb-8028-aae36f8d257d, stop 90bb4684-... (status=pending, order=2) — stop 1 in this route is already 'delivered' so does not render start-nav-btn. Tests run on mobile viewport 390x844 with Playwright context geolocation override (53.43, 14.55). RESULTS: (1) Idle: compass-bar NOT in DOM ✓, start-nav-btn visible ✓. (2) After tap start-nav-btn: compass-bar appears within ~3s with text '43 m  Lekko w prawo • Kaszubska' — distance pattern /\d+\s?(m|km)/ ✓, Polish label 'Lekko w prawo' from the documented set ✓, stop-nav-btn visible / start-nav-btn replaced ✓. (3) OSRM request to router.project-osrm.org/route/v1/driving/14.55,53.43;14.5528,53.4285?overview=full&geometries=geojson&steps=true was captured — steps=true present ✓. (4) pin-user blue marker present inside navigate-map iframe ✓. (5) Home page smoke loads ✓. (6) FAILURE on stop-nav-btn flow: tapping stop-nav-btn triggers TypeError 'LocationEventEmitter.removeSubscription is not a function' from expo-location subscription cleanup on web, the NavigateScreen component crashes and is recovered by React error boundary (LogBox). compass-bar disappears (because state resets) but start-nav-btn does NOT re-appear because LogBox overlay covers the bottom panel and/or the component is in error-recovery state. No raw page errors captured (errors caught by error boundary), but a clear console.error is emitted with full React stack referencing NavigateScreen. Deprecated 'shadow*' and 'props.pointerEvents' warnings still present from iteration 5.

ui_bugs.issue: Stopping navigation crashes the screen: useEffect cleanup calls sub.remove() on the expo-location LocationSubscription, which internally invokes LocationEventEmitter.removeSubscription — that API does not exist in the current expo-location web build. React error boundary catches it, but start-nav-btn does not become visible again after stop-nav-btn tap. Reference: /app/frontend/app/route/[id]/stop/[stopId]/navigate.tsx:144-160.

react_native_issues.issue: Deprecation warning at runtime: 'shadow*' style props are deprecated. Use 'boxShadow'. Reference: /app/frontend/app/route/[id]/stop/[stopId]/navigate.tsx:475-477, 496-500, 522-523.
Deprecation warning at runtime: props.pointerEvents is deprecated. Use style.pointerEvents instead. Reference: /app/frontend/app/route/[id]/stop/[stopId]/navigate.tsx:269 and 288.

action_items: FIX (HIGH): Wrap the LocationSubscription cleanup in try/catch to survive the expo-location web bug, e.g. `try { sub.remove(); } catch {}` at /app/frontend/app/route/[id]/stop/[stopId]/navigate.tsx:158. Alternatively only attach the subscription on native (Platform.OS !== 'web') and skip watchPositionAsync on web, since geolocation polling on web preview adds no value. This unblocks the stop-nav-btn flow.
Migrate styles.compassRow, styles.topBarRow, styles.bottom shadow* props to the cross-platform `boxShadow` string per RN 0.76+.
Move pointerEvents='box-none' from the two SafeAreaView elements (compassWrap, topBar) to style={{ pointerEvents: 'box-none' }}.
critical_code_review_comments: Compass distance uses the maneuver's END point coordinate (steps[stepIndex].maneuver.location). For an active depart step the distance shown is essentially 0 — implementation jumps stepIndex to 1 on first OSRM response which is correct, but consider treating the very first hint as 'Ruszaj' until the user is >25m from the depart point so the user sees the intended starting direction.

stepIndex auto-advance loop (lines 206-216) compares to steps[stepIndex].maneuver.location which is the START of that step (where the maneuver happens). Once user reaches it, idx++ advances to the next maneuver — correct, but the displayed 'road' on line 226 uses step.name (current road) which can briefly show the road just exited rather than the road being entered. Minor UX nit, not a bug.

success_rate.frontend: 5 of 6 sub-checks pass (idle ✓, compass content ✓, OSRM steps=true ✓, pin-user ✓, smoke ✓, stop-nav-btn ✗)

seed_data_creation: None. Used seeded route 4fca5350-ddf4-40cb-8028-aae36f8d257d, stop 90bb4684-7c86-4f7a-ae6d-5f1c25ce6e8f (order 2, pending).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Playwright context.grant_permissions(['geolocation']) + context.set_geolocation({...}) BEFORE page.goto works on the Expo web preview and triggers Location.getCurrentPositionAsync + watchPositionAsync correctly. Use stop 2 (90bb4684-...) because stop 1 is already delivered. OSRM responded fast in this run (single request, captured within ~3s) and steps were populated. The compass-bar test must look for both a distance regex and any Polish label substring from the documented set — the inner text contains the icon glyph + newline + distance + newline + label so use page.inner_text() not text content. The stop-nav-btn crash is reproducible 100%; after tapping it, query_selector('[data-testid="start-nav-btn"]') returns null because LogBox red overlay covers the bottom panel.
rca_of_issue: ROOT CAUSE: expo-location v18+ exposes Location.LocationSubscription whose .remove() method internally calls LocationEventEmitter.removeSubscription(). On the Expo Web build this function was removed (React Native Web no longer exposes removeSubscription on NativeEventEmitter; the modern API is subscription.remove() directly on the underlying EmitterSubscription). When the NavigateScreen useEffect on lines 144-160 returns its cleanup and calls sub.remove(), the expo-location wrapper throws TypeError. REPRODUCE: open /route/{id}/stop/{pending-stop-id}/navigate, grant geolocation, tap start-nav-btn (navigating=true → watchPositionAsync subscribed), then tap stop-nav-btn (navigating=false → cleanup runs sub.remove() → throws). MITIGATION: (a) try/catch around sub.remove(), or (b) guard with Platform.OS !== 'web', or (c) await Location.watchPositionAsync directly and skip the subscription removal on web. Recommended (a)+(b).
